metadata.name: simplesam
metadata.summary: Simple pure Python SAM parser and objects for working with SAM records

modules.name: simplesam
documentation:
Classes to handle alignments in the SAM format.

Reader -> Sam -> Writer
pylint: disable=E1101, dangerous-default-value

functions.name: simplesam.parse_sam_tags
documentation: Return a dictionary containing the tags

functions.name: simplesam.encode_tag
documentation:
Write a SAM tag in the format `TAG:TYPE:data`. Infers the data type
from the Python object type.

```pycon
>>> encode_tag('YM', '#""9O"1@!J')
'YM:Z:#""9O"1@!J'
```

functions.name: simplesam.decode_tag
documentation:
Parse a SAM format tag to a (tag, type, data) tuple. Python object
types for data are set using the type code. Supported type codes are: A, i, f, Z, H, B

```pycon
>>> decode_tag('YM:Z:#""9O"1@!J')
('YM', 'Z', '#""9O"1@!J')
>>> decode_tag('XS:i:5')
('XS', 'i', 5)
>>> decode_tag('XF:f:100.5')
('XF', 'f', 100.5)
```

functions.name: simplesam.tile_region
documentation:
Make non-overlapping tiled windows from the specified region in
the UCSC-style string format.

```pycon
>>> list(tile_region('chr1', 1, 250, 100))
['chr1:1-100', 'chr1:101-200', 'chr1:201-250']
>>> list(tile_region('chr1', 1, 200, 100))
['chr1:1-100', 'chr1:101-200']
```

functions.name: simplesam.bam_read_count
documentation: Return a tuple of the number of mapped and unmapped reads in a BAM file

classes.name: simplesam.Reader

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.next
documentation: Returns the next {class}`.Sam` object

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.__len__
documentation:
Returns the number of reads in an indexed BAM file.
Not implemented for SAM files.

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.subsample
documentation:
Returns an interator that draws every nth read from
the input file. Returns {class}`.Sam`.

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.header_as_dict
documentation: Parse the header list and return a nested dictionary.

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.seqs
documentation: Return just the sequence names from the @SQ library as a generator.

methods.name: simplesam.Reader.tile_genome
documentation: Return a generator of UCSC-style regions tiling `width`.
Read SAM/BAM format file as an iterable.

classes.name: simplesam.Writer

methods.name: simplesam.Writer.write
documentation: Write the string representation of the `sam` {class}`.Sam` object.
Write SAM/BAM format file from {class}`.Sam` objects.

classes.name: simplesam.Sam

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.__str__
documentation:
Returns the string representation of a SAM entry. Correspondes to one line
in the on-disk format of a SAM file.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.__len__
documentation:
Returns the length of the portion of `self.seq` aligned to the reference. Unaligned reads will
have len() == 0. Insertions (I) and soft-clipped portions (S) will not contribute to the aligned length.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(cigar='8M2I4M1D3M4S')
>>> len(x)
16
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.__getitem__
documentation:
Retreives the SAM tag named "tag" as a tuple: (tag_name, data). The
data type of the tag is interpreted as the proper Python object type.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(tags=['NM:i:0', 'ZZ:Z:xyz'])
>>> x['NM']
0
>>> x['ZZ']
'xyz'
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.__setitem__
documentation:
Stores the SAM tag named "tag" with the value "data". The
data type of the tag is interpreted from the Python object type.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(tags=[])
>>> x['NM'] = 0
>>> x['NM']
0
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.index_of
documentation: Return the relative index within the alignment from a genomic position 'pos'

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.gapped
documentation:
Return a {class}`.Sam` sequence attribute or tag with all
deletions in the reference sequence represented as 'gap_char' and all
insertions in the reference sequence removed. A sequence could
be :class:`Sam.seq`, `Sam.qual`, or any {class}`.Sam` tag that
represents an aligned sequence, such as a methylation tag for bisulfite
sequencing libraries.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(*'r001      99      ref     7       30      8M2I4M1D3M      =       37      39      TTAGATAAAGGATACTG       *'.split())
>>> x.gapped('seq')
'TTAGATAAGATA-CTG'
>>> x = Sam(*'r001      99      ref     7       30      8M2I4M1D3M      =       37      39      TTAGATAAAGGATACTG       *'.split(), tags=['ZM:Z:.........M....M.M'])
>>> x.gapped('ZM')
'............-M.M'
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.parse_md
documentation: Return the ungapped reference sequence from the MD tag, if present.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.cigars
documentation:
Returns the CIGAR string as a tuple.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(cigar='8M2I4M1D3M')
>>> x.cigars
((8, 'M'), (2, 'I'), (4, 'M'), (1, 'D'), (3, 'M'))
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.tags
documentation:
Parses the tags string to a dictionary if necessary.

```pycon
>>> x = Sam(tags=['XU:Z:cgttttaa', 'XB:Z:cttacgttaagagttaac', 'MD:Z:75', 'NM:i:0', 'NH:i:1', 'RG:Z:1'])
>>> sorted(x.tags.items(), key=lambda x: x[0])
[('MD', '75'), ('NH', 1), ('NM', 0), ('RG', '1'), ('XB', 'cttacgttaagagttaac'), ('XU', 'cgttttaa')]
```

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.paired
documentation:
Returns True if the read is paired and
each segment properly aligned according to the aligner.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.mapped
documentation: Returns True of the read is mapped.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.secondary
documentation: Returns True if the read alignment is secondary.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.reverse
documentation: Returns True if `Sam.seq` is being reverse complemented.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.passing
documentation: Returns True if the read is passing filters, such as platform/vendor quality controls.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.duplicate
documentation: Returns True if the read is a PCR or optical duplicate.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.coords
documentation: Returns a range of genomic coordinates for the query sequence positions in the gapped alignment.

methods.name: simplesam.Sam.safename
documentation: Return `Sam.qname` without paired-end identifier if it exists
Object representation of a SAM entry.
